2026-07-01.patch_summary: This patch removes the legacy ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() path in the iomap code, which is no longer needed since all in-tree filesystems have implemented the ->iomap_next() function. The patch updates the iomap_iter function to use the new ->iomap_next() function instead of the legacy functions. This change simplifies the code and reduces complexity.

reviews.author: Darrick Wong
reviews.summary: Reviewer Darrick Wong agreed that the legacy path can be removed and gave a Reviewed-by tag.
reviews.raw_body:
Yeah, the legacy path can go away. :)

Reviewed-by: "Darrick J. Wong" <djwong@kernel.org>

--D

2026-07-02.patch_summary: This patch removes the legacy ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() path in the iomap code, as all in-tree filesystems have implemented the newer ->iomap_next() method. The removal involves deleting related functions and code from the fs/iomap/iter.c file and updating the include/linux/iomap.h header file to reflect this change.
